channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump pardons anti-abortion activists who blockaded clinic entrances
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — President Donald Trump announced Thursday he would pardon anti-abortion activists convicted of blockading abortion clinic entrances. Trump called it “a great honor to sign this.” "They should not have been prosecuted," he said as he signed pardons for "peaceful pro-life protesters.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump revokes protections for former Secretary of State Pompeo and top aide threatened by Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has revoked government security protection for former Secretary of State Mike Pompeo and his top aide, Brian Hook, who have faced threats from Iran since they took hard-line stances on the Islamic Republic during Trump's first administration.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FEMA faces shakeup under Trump while it wrestles with disasters on both coasts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is preparing to reshape the Federal Emergency Management Agency, which has been on the frontlines of responding to recent wildfires in California and last year's hurricane in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England sentenced to over 50 years
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A teenager who stabbed three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England was sentenced Thursday to more than 50 years in prison for what a judge called “the most extreme, shocking and exceptionally serious crime.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump signs order directing release of JFK assassination files
item.description: The order directs the director of national intelligence and attorney general to present a plan within 15 days for the “full and complete release of records relating to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida officer accidentally shoots man with his own gun
item.description: A Florida police officer is without a job after accidentally discharging a man's gun during a traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KREX 5

item.title: Police review writings tied to Nashville school shooter who killed female student and himself
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — Authorities are examining “very concerning online writings and social media posts” connected to the shooter who killed a female student and wounded another student in a Nashville high school cafeteria.
item.dc:creator: KRISTIN M. HALL, TRAVIS LOLLER and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump order ending federal DEI programs leaves agencies and stakeholders on uncertain ground
item.description: Federal agencies, employees and contractors are trying to process how President Donald Trump’s sweeping anti-DEI executive order will upend their work.
item.dc:creator: Fernanda Figueroa, Ayanna Alexander And Corey Williams, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: USDA secretary nominee says politics shouldn't influence California wildfire response
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee to head the U.S. Department of Agriculture says efforts by the U.S. Forest Service to fight wildfires in California shouldn’t be influenced by politics.
item.dc:creator: Scott Mcfetridge, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Aerial attack helps firefighters maintain the upper hand on a huge fire north of Los Angeles
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Overnight water drops helped stop the spread of a huge wildfire churning through rugged mountains north of Los Angeles and firefighters battled to increase containment as dangerous winds whipped up again Thursday.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wall of ice the size of Rhode Island heading toward penguin-packed island off Antarctica
item.description: The world's biggest iceberg — a wall of ice the size of Rhode Island — is lumbering toward a remote island off Antarctica that's home to millions of penguins and seals.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: College enrollment now higher than pre-pandemic levels
item.description: Some of the biggest increases were in undergraduate certificate programs, with enrollments 28.5 percent above 2019 levels.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: California Legislature approves $2.5B for state response to Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: The California state Legislature has approved a more than $2.5 billion package to help the Los Angeles area recover from its recent deadly wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Austin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Homelessness nearly doubles in blue state in just 2 years: report
item.description: Homelessness in New York has dramatically worsened between 2022 to 2024. Asylum seekers are the main cause of the issue, accounting for 88% of the homeless.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dozens in Border Patrol custody after boat runs out of fuel near San Diego
item.description: A Coast Guard crew deployed a boarding team and was able to safely take 26 people representing multiple nationalities to shore
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, NewsNation

item.title: Murkowski announces she will vote against Hegseth
item.description: Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) announced Thursday afternoon she will vote against Pete Hegseth, President Trump’s nominee to serve as secretary of Defense, citing his past infidelity and allegations of sexual assault and excessive drinking.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What we know so far about the Antioch High School shooter
item.description: Solomon Henderson arrived at the school on a bus and is believed to have gone into the restroom and retrieved a pistol.
item.dc:creator: Alicia Patton, NewsNation

item.title: Alabama man charged with 11 homicides -- including two mass shootings -- pleads not guilty
item.description: An Alabama man charged with homicide in two separate mass shootings pleaded not guilty in state court on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about President Donald Trump's order targeting the rights of transgender people
item.description: An executive order President Donald Trump signed on his first day back in office offers a new federal government definition of the sexes that could have a major impact on transgender people nationwide.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and CARLA K. JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 planets to align in the night sky: Here's when
item.description: You might want to keep your eyes on the skies through next month: Six planets will align in January and February.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Purdue Pharma and owners to pay $7.4 billion in settlement of lawsuits over the toll of OxyContin
item.description: Members of the Sackler family who own OxyContin maker Purdue Pharma, and the company itself, agreed to pay up to $7.4 billion to settle lawsuits over the toll of the powerful prescription painkiller.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate panel overwhelmingly advances Trump pick for VA secretary
item.description: The Senate Veterans’ Affairs Committee on Thursday easily advanced former Georgia GOP Rep. Doug Collins's nomination to be the secretary of Veterans Affairs, setting him up for a full Senate vote likely to be uncontentious. The panel voted 18-1, with the lone "no" vote coming from Sen.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Senate GOP full steam ahead on Hegseth despite abuse allegations
item.description: Senate Republicans are steamrolling ahead on Pete Hegseth’s nomination to lead the Pentagon, and a new report detailing allegations of abusive behavior by the nominee have seemingly not dissuaded them.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: CNN is announcing layoffs as part of a further shift to digital business
item.description: CNN is expected to lay off about 200 of its employees, or 6% of its workforce, in part of a shift toward a more digital-oriented future.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump's geographic renaming plans leave mapmakers pondering what to do next
item.description: What's in a name change, after all? The water bordered by the Southern United States, Mexico and Cuba will be critical to shipping lanes and vacationers whether it’s called the Gulf of Mexico, as it has been for four centuries, or the Gulf of America, as President Donald Trump ordered this week.
item.dc:creator: LAURIE KELLMAN and HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Costco stands by its DEI policies as other companies scale back
item.description: Costco is pushing back on a shareholder proposal that urges the wholesale club operator to conduct an evaluation of any business risks posed by its diversity, equity and inclusion practices. Investors were expected to vote on the recommendation during the company's annual meeting Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: With Trump pivot back to pro-oil and gas policies, one renewable energy finds favor
item.description: As promised, President Donald Trump began reversing the country's energy policies his first day in office with a spate of orders largely favoring oil, gas and coal. But there is one renewable energy that did find favor: geothermal.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER McDERMOTT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump lists 'number of reasons' for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters
item.description: President Donald Trump listed a “number of reasons” for pardoning violent Jan. 6 rioters during an interview aired Wednesday night with Fox News’s Sean Hannity.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Las Vegas hotel workers union reaches deal with casino to end longest strike in decades
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — The longest strike in decades by Las Vegas hotel workers ended Wednesday with a new contract between the union and a casino where hundreds of employees had walked off the job in November.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says he may withhold federal aid for Los Angeles fires
item.description: The president repeated false claims that the state's fish conservation efforts in the northern part of the state are responsible for fire hydrants running dry in urban areas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? This is probably why
item.description: The official POTUS and White House accounts are managed by the White House and “change when the occupant of the White House changes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: U.S. government sues Southwest Airlines, fines Frontier for chronically delayed flights
item.description: “Airlines have a legal obligation to ensure that their flight schedules provide travelers with realistic departure and arrival times," Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Hughes Fire forces partial evacuation of 5,000+ person Los Angeles jail
item.description: The fast-moving Hughes Fire has forced the partial evacuation of a 5,000+ inmate jail in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs sues man who claimed to have incriminating sex tapes, says they didn't exist
item.description: Combs, 55, has pleaded not guilty to sex trafficking charges lodged against him after his September arrest.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Indiana couple abused 33 kids over 13 years: Docs
item.description: Sonja Stafford, 60, is charged with 62 criminal counts including neglect of a dependent, human labor trafficking, strangulation and more.
item.dc:creator: Deja Studdard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WFLA, NBC
